theme: Why your best employees are quietly updating their LinkedIn

linkedin.text:
Your best people are quietly updating their LinkedIn. Here's what to do about it.

Not the loud ones. Not the ones complaining at team meetings. The quiet ones. The ones who have delivered for years, who never ask for more, who just... do their jobs.

The research is consistent: top performers leave before they complain. They update their profiles, take a few calls, and one day they walk into your office and hand you a letter.

After 16 years working with Israeli and European companies on incentive programs, here's what I've learned about what actually retains top talent:

It's not salary. Salary gets them to come. It keeps them for 2 years.

It's not promotions. Promotions satisfy ambition. They don't create loyalty.

What retains top performers long-term is feeling seen. Publicly. In front of their peers. With something that money didn't just buy — with something that was designed specifically for them.

The incentive trip where their name is called in front of 60 colleagues. Where the CEO makes a speech about why this specific person's contribution mattered. Where they come back and their colleague asks "what did you do to get on that trip?"

That's retention.

The companies we work with that run annual incentive programs have an average voluntary turnover of 9% for their incentivized cohort.

The industry average for similar roles is 22%.

The numbers speak for themselves.

Is your organization running an incentive program for 2026? I'd be happy to discuss.

📩 alon@upe.co.il | 🌐 upe.co.il

#EmployeeRetention #TopTalent #IncentiveTravel #HRLeadership #CorporateEvents #Leadership #UproductionEvents #EmployeeEngagement #TalentManagement #RetentionStrategy #MICE
linkedin.cta: DM or visit upe.co.il to start planning
